events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: timing marks misaligned by a tooth. This is a timing & mechanical task in automotive powertrain. Timing marks misaligned by a tooth is out of tolerance - timing must be exactly aligned. This is a fail, critical risk because incorrect timing can cause engine damage (interference engine could have piston-to-valve contact). Component condition: installed-defective. Work stage: rough-complete. Percent complete maybe 70-80. Value: 1 (tooth misalignment). Confidence high. Findings: timing marks misaligned by one tooth. Actions: re-time the engine, realign timing marks, do not rotate engine until corrected. Rationale: timing marks must align exactly per OEM service info; one tooth off can cause valve timing errors and potential engine damage. References: OEM service info.
